title: neltuma fiebrigii
intro: Neltuma fiebrigii, commonly known as the Passion Flower of the Andes, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America. It has been used for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, due to its therapeutic actions as a sedative, anxiolytic, and antispasmodic. The bioactive constituents of Neltuma fiebrigii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Research suggests that these constituents may also possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to the plant's potential therapeutic applications.
side_effects: Neltuma fiebrigii can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Prolonged consumption of the plant's extracts has been linked to liver and kidney damage, as well as an increased risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties. In rare cases, excessive use of the plant may also lead to respiratory problems, including bronchitis and asthma-like symptoms. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using the Neltuma fiebrigii plant for health purposes to minimize the risk of these side effects.
